url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation. Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page. Each pick's competitor section includes 5 suggested queries + competitor-drift-watchpoint flags (A19: stale £10k threshold from 2022; stale 'property total gross' threshold-test framing; stale Form 17 backdating framing; stale SI 2021/1076 framing. A20: stale 2%/2%/4% MTD framing; stale £100 immediate penalty framing; stale SI 2021/1076 framing; stale LtdCos-in-MTD framing; stale inability-to-pay reasonable-excuse framing).
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §19 (MTD ITSA Wave 3 + Wave 4 extensions §19.1-§19.15) + §24 (Wave 5 spouse-mechanics framework) + §27 (HMRC enquiry + appeals architecture including §27.4 Perrin + §27.5 Martland + §27.9 do-not-write list) house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. F-3 drift catch on SI 2021/1076 → SI 2026/336 migration carried verbatim across both A19 + A20 (Stage 1 originated; Stage 2 confirms; full back-patch list at F-3 in flags doc; brief bodies cite SI 2026/336 throughout). Stage 1 verification on ITA 2007 s.836 + s.837 + SI 2026/336 regs 18/20/24/25/27 + FA 2021 Sch 24 paras 5(9)(b)/8(1)/8(4)(b)/15(2)/15(4) all 2026-05-27 against legislation.gov.uk. FA 2025 enacted-text equivalence to Spring Statement 2025 HTML figures (15/30/31 day-triggers at 3%/3%/10%) is an OPEN verification GAP carried forward from Stage 1 to RUN — Stage 2 surfaced the FA 2025 contents URL + flagged the gap as a §16.35 RUN write-time mandate. SI 2026/336 reg 19 + TMA 1970 Sch A1 para 14(2) verbatim verification deferred from Stage 1 to RUN.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified ITA 2007 s.836 (verbatim subsection (2) default 50/50 + Exceptions A-F at subsection (3)) + ITA 2007 s.837 (verbatim joint-declaration wording + 60-day notice window + prospective-only effect) + SI 2026/336 regs 18 (exclusion-notice exemption) + 20 (meaning of 'excluded') + 24 (three-year income-exemption exit) + 25 (qualifying income) + 27 (qualifying amount) + FA 2021 Sch 24 paras 5(9)(b) (4-point threshold) + 8(1) (dual-condition reset) + 8(4)(b) (12-month compliance period) + 15(2) (point per failure) + 15(4) (£200 at threshold) + Spring Statement 2025 HTML 3%/3%/10% on 15/30/31 day-triggers anchors at seed time on 2026-05-27. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §19 (full Wave 3 + Wave 4 extensions) + §24 (Wave 5 spouse-mechanics framework) + §27 (Wave 7 lock including §27.4 Perrin + §27.5 Martland + §27.9 do-not-write list) locks; no NEW drift catches at Stage 2 review beyond F-3 (already logged by Stage 1).

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 2 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch are unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (continuing the pattern observed in M3-A-B1 D-2 + M3-A-B2 D-2 + M3-A-B3 D-2 + M3-B-B1 D-2). A19 extension landed at +231 lines; A20 extension landed at +260 lines (worked examples 6-7 per pick + 12-14 FAQs per pick + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 2 author entry + §16.36 statutory cross-check entry + §16.31 URL verification entry + anti-templating watchpoints entry). Cross-batch consistency across MW3 confirms Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_MTD_JOINT_OWNER_VS_PENALTY_CATALOGUE_DISCIPLINE — picks 1 (A19 joint-owner deep-dive) and 2 (A20 practitioner-grade penalty + exemption catalogue) form a deliberate 2-page MTD sub-cluster within batch M3-A-B4, each targeting a distinct intent against the broader MTD architecture (A6 news-framing + A7 exit-mechanic + A11 LtdCo + A16 system-overhaul + A18 qualifying-income in prior batches or prior waves). Stage 2 anti-templating notes for both picks explicitly identify the sibling-collapse risk + structurally-distinct frames: A19 is joint-property-owner audience-specific overlay (ITA 2007 s.836 default 50/50 + s.837 Form 17 60-day window + per-spouse ASA + share-of-gross + tenants-in-common Declaration of Trust); A20 is practitioner-grade comprehensive catalogue (three-regime penalty matrix + comprehensive exemption catalogue + operational what-to-watch + appeals route). RUN session pairing should hold the line on audience-specific-overlay-vs-catalogue distinction.

D-4 STAGE2_F3_CROSS_PICK_CONSISTENCY — both picks depend on F-3 drift catch (SI 2021/1076 revoked 1 April 2026 by SI 2026/336). Stage 2 brief extensions carry the SI 2026/336 migration verbatim across both briefs (reg 25 qualifying income; reg 27 qualifying amount; reg 18 exclusion-notice exemption; reg 20 'excluded' definition; reg 24 three-year income-exemption exit). Cross-pick consistency anchor for RUN session: pages writing must cite SI 2026/336 going forward and reference SI 2021/1076 only in historical-context discussion.

D-5 STAGE2_FA2025_VERIFICATION_GAP_CARRIED_FORWARD — Stage 1 explicitly identified an OPEN verification gap on FA 2025 enacted-text equivalence to the Spring Statement 2025 HTML document figures for the accelerated 15/30/31 day-3%/3%/10% MTD ITSA late-payment schedule. Stage 2 surfaced the FA 2025 contents URL (legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2025/8/contents) into the A20 brief authority list + flagged the gap as a §16.35 RUN write-time mandate (twelfth-consecutive-Bill-vs-enacted-Act-drift watchpoint per F-6 / F-11 / F-12 / F-13 / F-18 / F-19 / F-20 program pattern). RUN session MUST close this gap at write per §16.35; Stage 2 cannot close upstream verification gaps that depend on enacted-text verification post-Royal-Assent (FA 2025 received Royal Assent at a date carrying the latency Stage 1 noted).

D-6 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 2 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B1 D-4 + M3-A-B2 D-5 + M3-A-B3 D-6 + M3-B-B1 D-5 patterns proportionally (smaller batch of 2 picks vs prior 6-pick batches; total ~25 minutes for 2 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-7 STAGE2_A19_ASYMMETRIC_SCOPE_BRIDGE_NOTE — A19 (joint-owner) and A20 (penalty + exemption catalogue) intersect at the asymmetric-scope edge case (one spouse in MTD scope, the other digitally excluded or not yet over threshold). Stage 2 brief extensions explicitly route this intersection: A19 covers the operational mechanics (per-spouse ASA, separate quarterly cycles, household bookkeeping discipline) with cross-link DOWNSTREAM to A20 for the digital-exclusion exemption deep mechanics. A20 covers the digital-exclusion exemption mechanics (SI 2026/336 reg 18 + reg 20 + TMA 1970 Sch A1 para 14(2)) with cross-link UPSTREAM to A19 for the joint-owner operational layer. RUN session should hold the bidirectional cross-link discipline; do not collapse asymmetric-scope coverage onto either page alone.

D-8 STAGE2_CROSS_BUCKET_CONSISTENCY_NOTE — A20 directly references §19.7 penalty-cascade content + the bifurcation across legacy + MTD regimes (already running through A13 SA penalty regime + A16 MTD overhaul + A18 qualifying-income from M3-A-B3 batch). RUN session should ensure the 15/30/31 day-triggers + 3%/3%/10% percentages on A20 are stated identically to A16 (and never paired with the legacy 31/46/91 day-triggers — the F-1-adjacent risk). A19 references §19.4 share-of-gross + §19.10 ASA per-owner authorisation + §19.13 letting-agent net-of-fees + §24 spouse-mechanics framework — RUN session should ensure consistency with §24 Wave 5 Session C operational pages on Form 17 documentary discipline (do not re-walk DoT execution mechanics; cross-link to §24-anchored pages).
anti_templating_check: Two briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Quality bar) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: A19 joint-owner deep-dive (vs A6 news / A7 exit / A11 LtdCo / A16 overhaul / A18 qualifying-income / A20 penalty-catalogue; ITA 2007 s.836 + s.837 + Form 17 60-day window + share-of-gross + per-spouse ASA is the differentiator; emphasis on prospective-only Form 17 effect + tenants-in-common distinction + asymmetric-scope operational layer); A20 practitioner-grade penalty + exemption catalogue (vs A6 news / A7 single-mechanic exit / A11 LtdCo / A13 general SA penalty regime / A16 overhaul / A18 qualifying-income / A19 joint-owner; three-regime catalogue + comprehensive exemption + operational what-to-watch + appeals route is the differentiator; clear MTD-vs-non-MTD penalty delimitation against A13; cross-link DOWNSTREAM to A7 for income-exit deep-dive). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; each worked-example persona surname distinct per pick (A19: Whitwell, Sheridan, Whitaker/Bennett, Marchand, Goodfellow; A20: Brennan, Holloway, Whitfield, Hartwell, Hartfield, Cartwright). 11 distinct anonymised personas across 2 picks; no surname collisions within batch; no surname collisions against prior MW3 batches (Pemberton/Whitfield/Cordwell/Marwood from M3-A-B3 A13; Hollingsworth/Rashleigh/Belmount/Vandenberg/Padworth from M3-A-B3 A14; Stratton/Underhill/Westhampnett/Hollybank/Penbury from M3-A-B3 A15; Ainsworth/Browning/Crowther/Davenport from M3-A-B3 A16; Kettering/Linstead/Mortlake/Norcombe/Ottershaw from M3-A-B3 A17; Pevensey/Quenington/Rochester/Stapleford/Thirling/Underbrook from M3-A-B3 A18). NOTE: A19 example uses 'Eleanor' (Sheridan) + A20 example uses 'Eleanor' (Whitfield AND Hartfield) — Eleanor recurs across 3 personas in the batch but with distinct surnames; RUN session may freshen names if cross-batch persona register feels saturated.
